execution_mode: scaffold-first; browser/runtime integration can be added later

starter_templates.java: public class Solution { public static boolean validate(String input) { return input != null; } }
starter_templates.python:
def validate(value):
    return value is not None
starter_templates.typescript: export function validate(value: unknown): boolean { return value !== null && value !== undefined; }

sample_exercises.title: PII for Banking observability and production readiness
sample_exercises.problem: Design, implement, test, or whiteboard a hard solution involving PII for Banking observability and production readiness. Explain assumptions, edge cases, validation strategy, quality risks, evidence expected, complexity, and production-readiness considerations.
sample_exercises.model_answer: For PII in a Banking context, I would first clarify the business workflow, data grain, systems involved, regulatory or operational risk, and the exact acceptance criteria expected from a Lead Quality Engineer. I would then design the validation approach across happy path, negative path, boundary conditions, integration points, data integrity, security/privacy, performance, and operational readiness. The automation strategy would separate fast checks for pull requests from deeper regression, data reconciliation, and non-functional suites that run on schedule or before release. Evidence would include executable test results, traceable requirements coverage, defect triage notes, logs, metrics, dashboards, API payload samples, database reconciliation results, and a go/no-go summary. Production readiness would require monitoring, rollback criteria, alert thresholds, test data controls, access controls, and support handoff.
hints: Start by clarifying scope, systems, data, and risk for PII in Banking.
Use a structure: assumptions, approach, validation, edge cases, evidence, readiness, risks.
followups: How would you automate this in CI/CD?
What evidence proves the release is ready?
Which production risks remain after testing?
How would you explain this to a business stakeholder?

rubric.name: Problem understanding
rubric.description: Clarifies scope, business context, constraints, and assumptions.

rubric.name: Technical approach
rubric.description: Presents a realistic design or implementation approach.

rubric.name: Edge cases and risks
rubric.description: Covers failure modes, boundary cases, negative scenarios, and quality risks.

rubric.name: Testing strategy
rubric.description: Defines unit, API, integration, data, performance, security, and regression validation.

rubric.name: Evidence and observability
rubric.description: Explains logs, metrics, traces, reports, screenshots, test evidence, and readiness signals.

rubric.name: Communication
rubric.description: Gives a structured answer suitable for a lead or architect interview.
